title: Sensitive File Commit Detection
dataSources: Push webhook with file path arrays, or GitHub compare API
spl:
index=devops sourcetype="github:webhook" event="push"
| mvexpand commits{}.modified limit=500
| where match(commits{}.modified,"(\.env|id_rsa|kubeconfig|\.pem|credentials\.xml)$")
| table _time, repository, commits{}.author.username, commits{}.modified
description: Paths such as `.env`, `id_rsa`, `kubeconfig`, and `*.pem` in commits indicate credential sprawl even before secret scanning fires.
value: Paths such as `.env`, `id_rsa`, `kubeconfig`, and `*.pem` in commits indicate credential sprawl even before secret scanning fires.
implementation: Expand commit file lists in ingestion. Alert on first match; auto-open rotation ticket. Pair with secret scanning (UC-12.1.4).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: GitHub `push` webhook payload (commits[].modified/added).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Push webhook with file path arrays, or GitHub compare API.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Expand commit file lists in ingestion. Alert on first match; auto-open rotation ticket. Pair with secret scanning (UC-12.1.4).

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=devops sourcetype="github:webhook" event="push"
| mvexpand commits{}.modified limit=500
| where match(commits{}.modified,"(\.env|id_rsa|kubeconfig|\.pem|credentials\.xml)$")
| table _time, repository, commits{}.author.username, commits{}.modified
```

Understanding this SPL

**Sensitive File Commit Detection** — Paths such as `.env`, `id_rsa`, `kubeconfig`, and `*.pem` in commits indicate credential sprawl even before secret scanning fires.

Documented **Data sources**: Push webhook with file path arrays, or GitHub compare API. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): GitHub `push` webhook payload (commits[].modified/added). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: devops; **sourcetype**: github:webhook. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=devops, sourcetype="github:webhook". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Expands multivalue fields with `mvexpand` — use `limit=` to cap row explosion.
• Filters the current rows with `where match(commits{}.modified,"(\.env|id_rsa|kubeconfig|\.pem|credentials\.xml)$")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Pipeline stage (see **Sensitive File Commit Detection**): table _time, repository, commits{}.author.username, commits{}.modified


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (sensitive paths), Bar chart (repos), Timeline.
grandmaExplanation: Paths such as, and in commits indicate credential sprawl even before secret scanning fires — so code changes that fail, leak secrets, or skip checks are caught early.
